dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for cardiac arrest.
Hi, my name is Anna. I'm an EMT. May I ask what prompted the call today?
We found the patient not breathing and unresponsive in the living room.
Let me assess the patient's responsiveness.
No eye opening, no verbal response, and no motor response.
The patient is pulseless and apneic. Airway must be secured immediately.
We're inserting an oropharyngeal airway to manage the airway.
No respiratory effort. She's apneic with respirations at 0.
No pulse. Manual CPR needs to begin now.
Applying AED lead. Patient is in asystole.
She was fully alert 20 minutes ago and in good spirits.
She has a history of breast cancer that returned and spread to her brain. She was in hospice care.
No documented DNR-family confirmed she is not a DNR.
She's unresponsive, so we cannot assess pain at this time.
No pulse detected in all major peripheral locations.
No spontaneous breathing observed.
No blood pressure reading-patient is pulseless.
Blood glucose level is 143.
SpO2 is 0 due to lack of spontaneous respirations.
Rhythm confirmed as asystole on the AED.
Starting manual CPR per EMS standing orders.
Oropharyngeal airway inserted and BVM ventilations initiated.
Intraosseous access placed-going to give 1:10,000 epinephrine.
Partner, ready to intubate using McGrath scope.
Intubated with a 7.0 ETT, confirming placement with waveform capnography.
Second dose of epinephrine administered via intraosseous at 18:14; no clinical response observed.
Rhythm remains asystole, no pulse, no breathing, and no signs of improvement.
Brief period of PEA noted shortly after starting resuscitation.
No signs of trauma noted during examination.
Confirming clear airway and continuing BVM with tube in place.
No eye movements, verbal response, or motor response-confirmed GCS of 3.
Noting second epinephrine dose at 18:14; no clinical response observed.
Rechecking patient status. Rhythm remains asystole with no signs of improvement.
No abrasions, bruises, or signs of injury on the body surfaces.
Confirmed airway with McGrath scope and bougie. Direct visualization of vocal cords noted.
A 7.0 endotracheal tube was intubated, secured, and remains in place with a tube holder.
No breath sounds noted post-intubation using waveform capnography.
No visible deformities or angulation on limbs or back.
Noting patient had terminal breast cancer with brain metastasis-on hospice, no DNR established.
Following protocol 2-7 as per standing orders for cardiac arrest.
Continuing manual compressions alongside BVM ventilations.
Patient secured to the stretcher in preparation for transport in place.
Based on current status, we will remain on scene following cessation of resuscitation.
No changes-patient remains in a pulseless and apneic state, rhythm asystole.
Confirmed asystolic rhythm with leads checked and rechecked by two providers.
Patient was last seen 20 minutes ago, active with family before unresponsive.
No blunt force injury or penetrating trauma evident through secondary exam.
Continuing compression and assisted ventilation as directed by protocol.
AED rhythm checks done per standard and not showing change from asystole.
Medication orders confirmed and repeated by physician on scene.
Blood glucose reconfirmed at 143, no change from earlier reading.
Patient not on any medications, as per family.
All interventions reviewed and performed as ordered.
Airway secured with 7.0 ETT, ongoing confirmation of airway patency.
No signs of spontaneous return of perfusion or respiration.
Oxygen saturation remains at 0 via pulse ox, all findings unchanged.
BVM ventilating via tube, compressions ongoing, medications administered.
Skin assessment completed-no findings that suggest trauma or changes in condition.
IO site confirmed in use, medications administered successfully.
Bougie passage through cords visualized for intubation confirmation.
Securement straps in place and patient tags properly applied.
No transport recommended given current status and physician decision.
Final vitals unchanged-0 pulse, 0 respirations, 0 blood pressure, asystole.
Final rhythm confirmation done by two trained rescuers, as per protocol.
Ending on-scene care for the patient immediately after resuscitation stopped.
Capnography device remains active showing no CO₂ present pre and post intubation.
Confirming time of last medication given and repeat orders.
Time stamp noted as final documentation for care provided today.
No masses or distention observed over suprapubic area.
Blood not drawn due to patient status and need for immediate control.
Beginning final checks on care provided to ensure preservation in paper and ePCR.
No known allergies per family, no patient information available.
OPA removed after successful intubation for better overall airway management.
No visible eye trauma or ocular discharge observed.
No patient reaction or response to stimulation or repeated interventions.
No measurable vital signs at all due to patient's current status.
Pupils unreactive to light; all signs confirm brain function absent.
No extremity swelling, fractures, or dislocations observed visually.
SpO2 device off given lack of hemodynamic changes and central perfusion.
No motor or verbal response to commands or sensory cues provided.
Finalizing records: breast cancer with brain metastasis, no DNR.
Deciding not to transport as per final field order and family acumen.
All medications and care entries are reflected in official EMS logs and paper.
Noting tags fully completed including time, associated care, and stabilization.
No jugular venous distension or evidence of neck trauma found.
No pulse, breath, or BP, as previously established and confirmed.
Ending all procedural activity upon command from EMS physician.
No fluid, bleeding, or discharge from ears observed.
Transport started is marked as no in official documentation.
No obstruction present; oropharynx clear after airway management.
All vitals remain unchanged from last pass with no progression or regression.
Chest no movement, no signs of trauma or structural compromise.
Patient left on scene in care of family per current orders.
Affirming the decision to remain on-scene for final recording.
Transport was not initiated; care concluded on scene per rationale.
Abdomen flat with no distress, rigidity, or internal mass found.
No new procedures required at this stage of resuscitation.
Spine assessed, no elevation of concern; movement unimpeded.
Justifying transport decision based on current scene and patient findings.
No puncture or vascular compromise throughout limbs or external surfaces.
Rechecking administration lines and access points to ensure correct use.
Overall skin condition appeared consistent with lack of perfusion and pallor.
No movement or waveforms in ECG tracings, confirming asystolic condition.
Confirming all field medications given are well coated in protocol and protocol followed.
Final note on scene safety and family support before completing documentation.
